escalation.rationale: US-Iran ceasefire extended but both sides warn readiness for war as peace talks remain uncertain in Pakistan.

ticker_items: Trump extends US-Iran ceasefire as peace talks stall in Pakistan
Both US and Iran warn readiness to resume war operations
Bulgaria elects pro-Russian ex-president Radev in landslide victory
Japan approves lifting ban on lethal weapons exports
French UNIFIL soldier killed in Lebanon ambush

claimed_totals.figure: 3,375 in Iran, 2,294 in Lebanon, 28 in Gulf states

middle_east.text: The United States and Iran both warn they are ready for war as a ceasefire deadline approaches
middle_east.speaker: Unknown
middle_east.title: Official statement

observers.description: Pro-Russian former president Rumen Radev wins parliamentary election in landslide victory. His center-left coalition emerged as clear winner, potentially pushing the EU and NATO member closer to Moscow. Radev had stepped down as president in January to run for parliament.

Parliament approves scrapping ban on lethal weapons exports, clearing final hurdles for postwar arms sales. The approval will facilitate future sales of next-generation fighter jets and combat drones. Move represents significant shift in Japan's pacifist defense posture.

Serving as mediator for US-Iran peace talks in Islamabad as ceasefire deadline approaches. White House reports VP JD Vance ready to fly back to Islamabad while Iran has not confirmed participation. Pakistan brokered the original ceasefire that took effect earlier.

financial.notes: Markets showing disconnect from Middle East war reality according to Washington Post analysis.

update_log.text: Trump extends US-Iran ceasefire until Iran presents proposal and talks conclude
Iran holds military parades in Tehran as original ceasefire deadline approached
French UNIFIL soldier killed in ambush while clearing explosives in southern Lebanon
Bulgaria's pro-Russian ex-president Radev wins parliamentary election by landslide
